reason: Could not construct a concrete refutation; the change is file-verified sound. (1) Fixture-edit claim is TRUE: tests/eval/fixtures/documentation/good/documented-public-function/change.patch adds count_items whose docstring promises 'the total number of items in the catalogue' but delegates to list_items(session) with no limit; the real template src/framework_cli/template/src/{{package_name}}/db/repository.py confirms list_items defaults to DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE=50 and clamps to MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100, so the count is silently bounded -> a genuine doc-vs-impl mismatch. All 3 baseline repeats (documentation/good/documented-public-function__r0/r1/r2) independently flagged exactly this on items.py and README.md, so the 'good' fixture is NOT clean and the re-author (true aggregate OR honest bounded docstring) correctly removes the over-flag bait rather than loosening anything. (2) No under-flagging of a real defect class: documentation is advisory (block_threshold None, thresholds.yaml fp_max 1.00, capped low/info, never high/medium) so it never gates a builder; suppressing nothing that could otherwise block. (3) No known-bad slips through: bad/undocumented-public-function is graded by file-presence (expect.json {file: src/demo/routes/items.py}), and baseline bad__r0 still surfaces low/low/low/info -> recall stays 1.00 after the change. (4) The scope fence (defer openapi.json regen->contracts, page-size-cap->performance, response_model/typing->api-design) matches the rubric's one-owner-per-class discipline and the contested page-size-cap boundary is eliminated by the re-author, so the good case is made cleaner, not dirtier; the bad case stays unambiguous. Per the verdict ADVISORY_LEAKY, the fp 1.00 is surfacing-by-design, not a real false positive, and the load-bearing fix is the good-fixture mismatch repair, which is correct.
